# FRAMEWORK PDF CAPTIONS — bgd-flooding 2025-04-25
# framework_doc: https://www.unocha.org/attachments/b9e1bf90-a96b-490f-bfc7-a31e98de669d/Bangladesh%20AA%20Framework%20-%20Flood%20-%20April%202025%20-%20FINAL%20.pdf
# pages: 14 · model: sonnet · via: claude headless
# Auto-generated descriptions of the PDF's visuals (maps/charts/tables). PUBLIC.
------------------------------------------------------------
--- page 1 ---
Cover + Executive Summary. Framework: Bangladesh Monsoon Floods AA Framework, 2025 Version. Approved by RC on 7 April 2025; endorsed by USG/ERC on 25 April 2025. BDRCS pioneered AA for monsoon floods in 2015; first OCHA-coordinated framework activated in 2020 and again in 2024 (disbursed $6.2M CERF + $2.3M partner funds). 2025 version expands forecasting, coordination, and funding for Jamuna and Padma basins. Key point: executive summary of Bangladesh flood AA framework with approval details and activation history.

--- page 2 ---
Table of contents and start of Introduction section. Structure: Executive Summary, Introduction (Risk/Hazard, Anticipatory Action), The Model (Forecasts, Triggers, Activation protocol, Improvements/Challenges), The Delivery (Overview, Activities, Collective improvements), The Money (Pre-arranged financing, CERF, Challenges), The Learning (M&E, Independent Evaluation), Final provisions, Annexes (one-page overview, draft trigger messages, priority intervention areas). Introduction: framework last activated July 2024 disbursing $6.2M CERF + $2.3M partners; 2025–2026 seasons supported by up to $6M CERF + $3,993,055 co-financing. Key point: document contents and financing baseline figures.

--- page 3 ---
Risk/Hazard, Anticipatory Action, and Triggers sections. Risk: Bangladesh monsoon flooding April–September, peaks June–September; ~25% country inundated in average year; severe flood every 4–5 years (1-in-5-year return period = threshold). Two-step trigger system: GloFAS (probabilistic, readiness warning, v3.1) + FFWC Bangladesh (deterministic, action trigger).

TRIGGER TABLE:
| Trigger | Jamuna River | Padma River |
|---|---|---|
| Readiness | Bahadurabad gauging station; 3 consecutive days forecasted by GloFAS; max 15-day lead time; >50% likely to cross 1-in-5-year return period | Baruria transit near Goalando station; 3 consecutive days; GloFAS 12–15-day lead time; >50% likely to cross 1-in-5-year return period |

Key point: defines readiness trigger thresholds for both river basins using GloFAS probabilistic model.

--- page 4 ---
Action triggers and Activation protocol table. Action trigger details:

| Trigger | Jamuna | Padma |
|---|---|---|
| Action | FFWC 5-day model forecasts Bahadurabad crossing "Danger Level" + 0.85 m; GloFAS/RIMES show sustained/increasing trend ≥3 consecutive days | FFWC 5-day model (3–5-day lead) forecasts Mawa station crossing 1-in-5-year return period ("Danger Level" + 0.5 m); GloFAS/RIMES sustained/increasing trend ≥3 consecutive days from day danger level forecast to be crossed |

ACTIVATION PROTOCOL TABLE (Who / What / How / When):
- OCHA-led forecast monitoring team: monitors GloFAS + FFWC; populates forecast log twice daily; from June
- OCHA-led forecast team (Hassan): confirms 15-day readiness OR 5-day action trigger; communicates to RC/RCO, UN agencies, partners by email + WhatsApp; as triggers are reached
- CERF: sends approval letters immediately when readiness trigger issued (or action trigger without prior readiness); when triggers reached
- RC/RCO: may call meeting with BDRCS, CARE, Save the Children, Cross/Red Crescent, WFP, FAO, UNFPA, UNICEF, OCHA, government (MoDMR); as applicable

Key point: defines action trigger thresholds (danger level + fixed offsets) and the full activation protocol chain.

--- page 5 ---
Improvements since 2020 / Challenges, and start of Delivery section. Improvements: (1) Padma River basin added; (2) GloFAS updated from v2.2 to v3.1 — Jamuna readiness trigger successful pre-activations doubled, fewer false/missed activations; readiness lead time extended from 10 to 15 days. Challenges: FFWC has negative bias (underprediction) at longer lead times and high water levels — may cause missed action triggers; more river basins under consideration. Delivery overview: households in Jamuna/Padma basins at risk of losing lives, homes, WASH access, assets, food, and income; interventions must be delivered within the window between trigger and peak flooding. WFP + BDRCS: 5,000 Taka each via mobile transfer (bKash) or post office; FAO: animal feed + flood-proof storage. Key point: GloFAS v3.1 upgrade doubled readiness trigger skill; unconditional cash (5,000 Taka) is core delivery component.

--- page 6 ---
Delivery activities continued + geographic coverage map. UNICEF: jerricans, water purification tablets, mobile water treatment units (Jamuna + Padma); GBV/SRH dignity kits + menstrual health support (Padma). UNFPA: dignity kits, MHH kits, cash/voucher assistance, CWSV volunteers, baby kits for pregnant women, SRH mobile camps. Start Network: separate threshold — 1-in-5-year return period with 70% confidence, 10-day forecast, ≥3 days at district level.

MAP: "Bangladesh Coordinated Anticipatory Action for Monsoon River Flooding"
- Shows Bangladesh with two sets of highlighted districts
- Jamuna basin (darker): Bogra, Jamalpur, Sirajganj, Gaibandha, Kurigram (5 districts, north-central corridor along Jamuna/Brahmaputra)
- Padma basin (lighter): Madaripur, Manikganj, Shariatpur (3 districts, south of Dhaka)
- Gauging stations marked: Bahadurabad (Jamuna, north), Goalando/Baruria (Padma, south of Dhaka)
- Total: 38 Upzilas across 8 districts

Key point: map shows 8 target districts across Jamuna and Padma basins with gauging station locations.

--- page 7 ---
Collective improvements: joint targeting/beneficiary database, joint early warning, localization, challenges, and start of pre-arranged financing. Joint targeting: common database of pre-verified poor/vulnerable households from most flood-prone unions. Joint early warning: messages jointly developed and tested (used in 2024 activation); WFP complements government system down to union level via community volunteers, mosques, radio, TV cable, SMS. Localization: BDRCS, CARE, Save the Children, Start Fund Bangladesh all involved; UN agencies work with local partners via CERF. Government engagement: MoDMR-led Forecast Based Financing/Action Task Force. Pre-arranged financing: up to $6M from CERF; co-financing >$3,993,055 (WFP, BDRCS, BRAC, Concern Worldwide, Start Network, Save the Children). Key point: collective improvements include joint beneficiary database, coordinated EWS, and $6M CERF with >$4M co-financing.

--- page 8 ---
Financing overview table and CERF section.

FINANCING TABLE (by agency, basin, source):
| Agency | Jamuna CERF | Jamuna Co-fund | Padma CERF | Padma Co-fund | Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| FAO | $400,000 | - | $195,640 | - | $595,640 |
| UNFPA | $630,000 | - | - | - | $630,000 |
| UNICEF | $300,000 | - | $277,000 | - | $577,000 |
| WFP | $3,000,000 | $1,400,000 | $1,200,000 | $600,000 | $6,200,000 |
| BDRCS | - | - | - | - | $441,950 |
| BRAC | - | - | - | - | $22,097 |
| CARE | - | - | - | - | $16,900 |
| Concern Worldwide | - | - | - | - | $250,000 |
| Start Network | - | - | - | - | $37,108 |
| Save the Children | - | - | - | - | $1,125,000 |
| (unnamed) | - | - | - | - | $100,000 |
| **Total** | $4,330,000 | $1,400,000 | $1,672,640 | $600,000 | **$9,995,695** |

CERF: ERC pre-agreed up to $6M; disbursed on no-regrets basis once 3 conditions met: RC endorsement, ERC pre-approval, and trigger activation within 2 years of pre-approval. Readiness funds released at readiness trigger; action funds released at action trigger. Key point: total financing ~$10M; WFP largest recipient ($6.2M combined CERF + co-fund).

--- page 9 ---
CERF challenges, The Learning section, M&E, Independent Evaluation. CERF challenge: "start-up/build costs" (common targeting, coordination infrastructure) not funded by CERF. Learning: ad-hoc learning committee convened by OCHA; pre-identified M&E questions: timing effectiveness, joint targeting impact, early warning effectiveness, localization, government partnership, agency action quality, AA effectiveness in reducing shock impact, AAP community participation. M&E: each agency uses existing systems; findings shared with OCHA; common questionnaire across all interventions. Independent evaluation: agencies agree to participate (as in 2020); ad-hoc committee oversees. Key point: narrative text on learning framework and M&E structure; no figures.

--- page 10 ---
Final provisions — Advocacy and public communication. Agencies coordinate key messages under RC leadership across development, activation, and learning phases. On activation: joint press release + social media amplification. Agencies share photos, videos, and impact stories with the ad-hoc committee. Key point: narrative text, no figure.

--- page 11 ---
Annex — One-page overview of framework. Structured summary table combining Reach, Trigger, and Agency/Activity/Financing for both basins.

Reach — Jamuna: Bogra, Jamalpur, Gaibandha, Kurigram, Sirajganj; Padma: Madaripur, Manikganj, Shariatpur.
Triggers (repeated in condensed form — same thresholds as pages 3–4).
Agency/Activity/Financing summary:
| Agency | Activity | Jamuna CERF | Jamuna Other | Padma CERF | Padma Other |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| WFP | Cash distribution | $3,000,000 | $1,400,000 | $1,200,000 | $600,000 |
| FAO | Animal feed; waterproof storage silos | $400,000 | - | $195,640 | - |
| UNICEF | Jerricans, water purification tablets, mobile water treatment plants | $300,000 | - | $277,000 | - |
| UNFPA | Dignity kits, cash support, menstrual health, voucher support, baby kits, reproductive health kits, SRH mobile camps | $630,000 | - | - | - |
| Total | | $4,330,000 | $1,400,000 | $1,672,640 | $600,000 |

Key point: consolidated one-page summary table of geographic reach, triggers, and financing by agency.

--- page 12 ---
Annex — Draft trigger messages. Distribution list table: To RC (Gwyn Lewis); cc: RCO (Aleyda Valdes, H.M. Shahid Hassan); OCHA (Michael Jensen, Daniel Pfister, Yakubu Alhassan, Regina Omlor, Nicolas Rost, Madoka Koide, Victoria Saiz-Omenaca, Sanjana Quazi, Safiyah Muyeen, Andrew Hyslop, Maya MANOCSOC, Ioannis Kaffes); FAO (Dia Sanou, Imanun Nabi Khan, Xiao Yang, Shamsun Naima Rahman, Azzizun Nahar Tania, Md. Mehedi Hasan Saddam); UNICEF (Antonio Franco Garcia, Lawrence Oduma, Mst Saleha Khatun, Abdullah Al Razwan); UNFPA (Masaki Watable, Rumana Khan, Murshida Akther); WFP (Riccardo Suppo, Manaan Mumma, Siddiqul-Islam Khan, Niger Dilnahar). Draft readiness trigger email: subject "Anticipatory action Bangladesh – Readiness trigger reached for [Jamuna/Padma]"; body states Bahadurabad/Baruria discharge forecasted >50% likely to cross 1-in-5-year return period (Jamuna threshold: 100,000 m³/s). Key point: draft readiness trigger notification email template with named distribution list and specific discharge threshold of 100,000 m³/s for Jamuna.

--- page 13 ---
Annex — Draft trigger messages continued. Action trigger email template: subject "Anticipatory action – Action trigger reached for [Jamuna/Padma]". Jamuna action trigger: FFWC 5-day model forecasts Bahadurabad crossing "Danger Level" + 0.85 m over 3 consecutive days. Padma action trigger: FFWC 5-day model (3–5-day lead) forecasts Mawa station crossing 1-in-5-year return period ("Danger Level" + 0.5 m); GloFAS/RIMES show sustained/increasing trend at Baruria transit for ≥3 consecutive days from day danger level forecast to be crossed. Key point: draft action trigger notification email with specific threshold values (Jamuna: DL+0.85 m at Bahadurabad; Padma: DL+0.5 m at Mawa).

--- page 14 ---
Annex — Priority intervention areas for coordinated anticipatory action. Full table of targeted Upazila-level admin units with p-codes.

Padma basin Upazilas (6):
- Madaripur / Shib Char — BD305487
- Manikganj / Harirampur — BD305628
- Manikganj / Shibalaya — BD305678
- Shariatpur / Bhedarganj — BD308614
- Shariatpur / Naria — BD308665
- Shariatpur / Zanjira — BD308694

Jamuna basin Upazilas (32 listed):
Bogra: Sonatola BD501095, Sariakandi BD501081, Dhunat BD501027
Jamalpur: Islampur BD453929, Dewanganj BD453915, Bakshiganj BD453907, Sarishabari BD453985, Melandaha BD453961, Jamalpur Sadar BD453936, Madarganj BD453958
Gaibandha: Palashbari BD553267, Fulchhari BD553221, Sundarganj BD553291, Gaibandha Sadar BD553224, Gobindaganj BD553230, Sadullapur BD553282, Saghatta BD553288
Kurigram: Raumari BD554979, Nageshwari BD554961, Bhurungamari BD554906, Ulipur BD554994, Rajarhat BD554977, Char Rajibpur BD554908, Chilmari BD554909, Kurigram Sadar BD554952
Sirajganj: Shahjadpur BD508867, Belkuchi BD508811, Chauhali BD508827, Kazipur BD508850, Sirajganj Sadar BD508878, Royganj BD508861, Ullah Para BD508894

Key point: complete p-coded Upazila list for 38 priority intervention areas across both basins.
